Everything looks exactly right. Here's a summary of all the changes made to `src/pipelines.js`:

---

### Changes made to `src/pipelines.js`

**1. Import** (line 44) — Added `AutoModelForImageTextToText` to the `models.js` import block alongside the other `AutoModel*` classes.

**2. Pipeline class** (lines 2008–2080) — Added `ImageTextToTextPipeline` immediately after the existing `ImageToTextPipeline`. The class follows the same patterns as the other multimodal pipelines:

| Design decision | Rationale |
|---|---|
| Extends `Pipeline` via `TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs` | Gives access to `this.model`, `this.tokenizer`, and `this.processor` — exactly what VLMs need |
| `_call(images, texts, generate_kwargs={})` | Takes both modalities as separate params; `generate_kwargs` matches `ImageToTextPipeline` and `DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipeline` |
| Iterates images one-at-a-time | Avoids padding complexity; mirrors how `ImageToTextPipeline` loops over `pixel_values` |
| `this.processor(image, text)` | VLM processors (LLaVA, SmolVLM, PaliGemma…) are designed to accept image + text together and return combined `input_ids`/`pixel_values` |
| `outputIds.slice(null, [inputLength, null])` | Removes the input-prefix tokens before decoding — the standard VLM generation pattern, validated in the LLaVA test suite |
| `isBatched = Array.isArray(images)` | Single image → `ImageTextToTextOutput`; array of images → `ImageTextToTextOutput[]` — consistent with `ImageToTextPipeline` |

**3. `SUPPORTED_TASKS` entry** (lines 3332–3341) — Registered as `"multimodal"` type with `AutoTokenizer`, `AutoModelForImageTextToText`, and `AutoProcessor`, defaulting to `HuggingFaceTB/SmolVLM-256M-Instruct` (a small, publicly available VLM).

**4. `pipeline()` JSDoc** — Added `"image-text-to-text": will return a ImageTextToTextPipeline` to the task list.
